name: cacao
description: Rust bindings to Apple's AppKit (macOS-only, native Cocoa)

license.comment: Dual-licensed MIT OR MPL-2.0+ (latter with version-upgrade clause).

documentation.comment: No dedicated documentation site; docs.rs is the primary reference.

latest-stable-version.comment: 0.4.0-beta2 (2023-08-01) is the newest published version but is a prerelease; max_stable is 0.3.2. Checked 2026-04-24.

maintenance-status.comment: Most recent push: 2025-02-03; most recent commits: 2024-12-28 (several PRs merged). No release since 2023-08 but trunk still receives occasional fixes. Calling it passive rather than active given long intervals between bursts of activity.

maturity.comment: README and docs.rs explicitly describe AppKit support as 'beta quality, fairly usable' and UIKit as 'alpha quality'. No 1.0 release.

corporate-backing.comment: Independent open-source project by Ryan McGrath; no corporate sponsor disclosed.

implementation.comment: Wraps Apple's Objective-C AppKit/UIKit via objc2/block2; control objects are Rust structs bridged to NSObject subclasses.

rendering-mode.comment: Inherits AppKit/UIKit's retained-tree model; views and controls are persistent stateful objects backed by Core Animation layers.

reactivity-model.comment: Uses the Cocoa delegate pattern: controls accept Rust trait-object delegates that receive callbacks from bridged Objective-C subclasses. State is mutated directly on retained widgets.

binding-target.comment: AppKit (Cocoa) on macOS plus UIKit on iOS/tvOS. The 'cocoa' tag is used here as the closest match in the attribute tag set.

thread-model.comment: Inherits AppKit/UIKit's main-thread-only UI constraint; no Send/Sync guarantees exposed for widget types.

renderer.comment: Delegates all drawing to AppKit/UIKit (Core Animation / Quartz). Under the hood Apple uses Metal but the library does not expose a GPU API to user code.

gpu-acceleration.comment: Views are layer-backed (Core Animation), which is GPU-composited by AppKit. Not user-configurable.

hidpi-support.comment: Handled automatically by AppKit/UIKit (Retina scaling is a framework responsibility, not cacao's).

vector-rendering.comment: Available via AppKit drawing primitives and CoreGraphics; cacao exposes a custom-drawing example but does not add its own vector-graphics API beyond the native one.

custom-shaders.comment: No Metal/Vulkan shader injection API in cacao itself. Users would drop to the Objective-C runtime to access Metal directly.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: Not explicitly provided as a cacao abstraction. Custom NSView drawing is demonstrated in examples/custom_image_drawing.rs, but embedding a wgpu/Metal viewport would require dropping to the objc escape hatch; no first-class support documented.

windows.comment: Apple-only (AppKit/UIKit). Windows is explicitly out of scope.

linux-x11.comment: GNUStep and Airyx are mentioned aspirationally in the crate description but are not officially tested platforms; treat Linux as unsupported.

ios.comment: UIKit bindings exist but are described as alpha quality; widget coverage is limited vs. AppKit.

android.comment: No Android target; bindings are Apple-specific.

web-wasm.comment: Not applicable; AppKit/UIKit cannot run in a browser.

embedded-no-std.comment: Links to Apple frameworks and libc; no no_std support.

authoring-style.comment: Builder-style imperative API mirroring AppKit's instantiate-and-configure pattern; no DSL or macro-based markup.

hot-reload.comment: No hot-reload mechanism; UI changes require recompile.

layout-system.comment: Auto Layout (NSLayoutConstraint wrapper) is the default; manual frame positioning is also available via frame_layout example. No flexbox/grid abstractions.

styling-approach.comment: Styling follows AppKit conventions (system colors, appearance inheritance) with per-view property setters. No CSS-like or stylesheet DSL layer.

theme-switching.comment: Inherits AppKit's automatic light/dark mode handling; the architecture doc specifically discusses dark-mode-aware ivar storage for background colors.

accessibility.comment: Accessibility is inherited from AppKit/UIKit (NSAccessibility). No dedicated cacao-level accessibility API or AccessKit integration was found in the docs.rs index.

internationalization.comment: RTL, IME, and locale-aware text shaping are handled by the underlying AppKit/UIKit text system. cacao itself does not add localization helpers — strings are passed through unchanged — but the OS takes care of rendering and input.

widget-breadth.comment: Covers common controls (Button, Label, TextField, ListView, Image, WebView, Toolbar, SplitView) but lacks tables, tree/outline views, rich text editing, charts, and advanced dialogs. Menu support is limited to one level deep. Below 'common widgets well-covered' threshold.

data-grid-table.comment: ListView (wraps NSTableView in list-mode) is available but no multi-column data-grid/table widget is exposed. NSOutlineView / column-based NSTableView are not wrapped.

rich-text-markdown.comment: AttributedString exists for simple styled text, but no markdown widget or rich-text editor (NSTextView wrapper is not exposed).

tree-outline.comment: No NSOutlineView wrapper in the public API.

native-menus.comment: NSMenu/NSMenuItem wrappers are available for the app menu bar. Noted limitation: currently only one level deep. Context menus are not explicitly documented.

file-dialogs.comment: FileSavePanel and FileSelectPanel wrap NSSavePanel / NSOpenPanel.

charts-plotting.comment: No first-party chart widget; no Apple SwiftUI Charts-equivalent binding.

custom-widgets.comment: Authoring a custom control requires subclassing via the objc runtime escape hatch or implementing a Delegate trait and understanding the bridge pattern. Non-trivial boilerplate and some unsafe exposure.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: No async runtime integration. The event loop is AppKit/UIKit's (NSRunLoop / UIApplication). Users bridging to tokio/async-std must do so manually.

system-tray.comment: No NSStatusItem/NSStatusBar wrapper found in the docs.rs item index. Could be accessed via the objc escape hatch, but not first-class.

notifications.comment: user_notifications module wraps UNUserNotificationCenter (opt-in via the 'user-notifications' feature flag).

drag-and-drop.comment: DragInfo / DragOperation types exposed; integrates with AppKit's drag-and-drop APIs.

embedded-webview.comment: webview module wraps WKWebView (opt-in via the 'webview' feature). Not available on tvOS (Apple restriction).

multi-window.comment: Multiple NSWindow instances supported; WindowController wrapper is provided.

binary-size.comment: No published hello-world binary-size measurement found. Given it is a thin Objective-C shim with AppKit linked dynamically, binaries are expected to be small, but not measured for this pass.

startup-time.comment: No cold-start measurements published. AppKit launch is typically fast but not independently measured here.

idle-redraw.comment: Inherits AppKit's event-driven redraw: views invalidate (setNeedsDisplay) and the runloop redraws on demand — not continuous.

dependency-count.comment: 14 direct dependencies per lib.rs; transitive count is well under 50 (objc2/block2 + core-foundation + a handful of small utilities). No heavy transitive graph.

documentation-quality.comment: API docs on docs.rs are present and reasonable, and there is a 2021 blog post motivation piece, but no book or structured tutorial. Many modules have terse descriptions; no migration docs.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic where possible (typed delegates, builder-ish config) but the objc bridge leaks (generic delegate type parameters, occasional unsafe escape hatch, Objective-C naming conventions). Functional but verbose.

examples-count.comment: 19 items (13 standalone .rs files + 4 example projects: browser, calculator, ios-beta, todos_list). Lands in the 10-20 'good' band.

interactive-playground.comment: No online playground/demo gallery exists for cacao.

ide-tooling.comment: Standard rust-analyzer is all that applies; no cacao-specific LSP, inspector, or formatter.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: No bundled widget-tree inspector. Users can fall back to Xcode's view-debugger at the Objective-C level, but cacao does not ship its own.

last-release.comment: 0.4.0-beta2 (prerelease). Latest stable 0.3.2 is older. No releases since 2023 despite continued trunk activity.

notable-production-users.comment: The maintainer's own 2021 post notes he doesn't expect widespread adoption. No widely-known shipping apps attribute themselves to cacao publicly.

community-size.comment: No Discord/Matrix; maintainer-directed support is 'twitter or email'. Fork count (79) and occasional PR contributors, but development is essentially solo.

ecosystem-crates.comment: No notable third-party theme, widget, or integration crates built specifically on top of cacao.
